Satoshi Nakamoto published the Bitcoin white paper on October 31, 2008, just weeks before the global financial system teetered on the brink of collapse. It was no accident that Bitcoin was born against the backdrop of massive bailouts, emergency relief, and a crisis of confidence in financial institutions. Bitcoin's creation was a response to the existing system—a system in which governments and central banks, when their structures became too fragile to withstand discipline, could always increase the money supply, expand guarantees, and socialize losses. The symbolic significance of Bitcoin's birth further illustrates this point. On January 3, 2009, the Bitcoin network's genesis block was mined, embedding a newspaper headline about the second round of bank bailouts in the UK. Whether you interpret it as a protest, a timestamp, or both, the message is clear: Bitcoin was born in the shadow of a monetary order reliant on intervention and bailouts. Now, let's turn our attention to the present. The US faces not only inflationary fears, but also the compounding problem of a credit cycle. Growth is more fragile, job growth has stagnated, fiscal conditions are far worse than in the 1970s, and inflationary pressures originate from areas the Federal Reserve cannot directly address. This precisely exposes the limits of a discretionary fiat currency management system. Central banks can be hawkish, but in an economy with debt reaching 122% of GDP, if forced to choose between protecting employment and suppressing supply-side-driven inflation, the market should judge that the threshold for this round of easing will be lower than in previous cycles. This logic of Bitcoin doesn't require hyperinflation to hold. It only needs a world where the market increasingly believes that each round of anti-inflationary action will be shorter, each easing cycle will arrive earlier, and each high-debt recession will force policymakers to return to easing. Ultimately, Bitcoin is the culmination of a century of human attempts to circumvent the Great Depression and suppress Schumpeterian innovation deflation. We've traded creative destruction for a highly financialized predicament—stock markets can't afford to fall, debt constrains monetary policy, exponential technological growth erodes jobs from within, and the rise of intelligent agents will permanently alter the workforce structure. This is why Bitcoin was created. Not because inflation is ever-present, but because the structure of the modern government financial system makes it difficult for hard currency to sustain itself in a painful environment. Crucially, this macroeconomic predicament arrives just as alternative infrastructure is maturing. Financial regulatory frameworks are well-established, and Wall Street ETFs provide barrier-free entry for ordinary investors. Traditional markets are facing a growing liquidity crisis, evidenced by private credit funds imposing redemption restrictions; while digital alternatives are rapidly developing. The surge in stablecoin trading is reshaping the global clearing system, and asset tokenization is fundamentally upgrading traditional financial infrastructure. This contrast is particularly stark when combined with the rapidly expanding digital economy—where intelligent agents will increasingly execute financial decisions autonomously. Bitcoin was designed because we needed a better system, and now, for the first time, the underlying infrastructure for that system is fully ready. The initial DOGE plan launched by governments failed because it only superficially and dramatically addressed the symptoms without ever addressing the root cause. The real D.O.G.E. problem is far more serious: debt, oil, growth, and jobs. This is the Fed's next dilemma. But this time, the entire system is heavily indebted and cannot withstand substantial tightening; asset bubbles are severe and cannot tolerate a genuine clearing; the job market is weak and insufficient to support a new round of comprehensive anti-inflation warfare; and political pressure is immense, preventing the Fed from making independent decisions. This is where Bitcoin's value lies. It was designed to address a moment when the market finally realizes that nations can no longer combat every inflationary shock in a credible, consistent, and pain-bearing manner. In the world of D.O.G.E., Bitcoin is no longer a speculative sideline, but an inevitable choice for the monetary system.

data.title: US Republicans propose cryptocurrency mining bill; Trump hopes to reach an agreement by April 6.
data.description:
Headline

▌US Republicans Introduce "Mined in America Act" to Promote Mining Expansion and Strengthen Bitcoin Reserves

On March 31, US Senators Cynthia Lummis and Bill Cassidy introduced the "Mined in America Act," aiming to promote the development of the cryptocurrency mining industry and enshrine President Trump's executive order establishing a strategic Bitcoin reserve into the legal system. The bill plans to strengthen the role of cryptocurrency mining in the US economy while consolidating previous policy arrangements regarding a strategic Bitcoin reserve through legislation. This reserve primarily consists of Bitcoin seized by the US Treasury Department through law enforcement. The bill also requires the US Department of Commerce to establish a voluntary certification system to regulate mining pools and farms. Furthermore, certified mining farms must gradually reduce their reliance on mining equipment produced by companies associated with "foreign adversaries." The proposers stated that this bill is one of the important measures to promote the US as a global digital asset center and aims to enhance the country's strategic position in the cryptocurrency industry chain.

▌White House: Trump Hopes to Reach Agreement with Iran Before April 6

On March 31, White House Press Secretary Levitt stated on the 30th that President Trump hopes to reach an agreement with Iran before the April 6 deadline. Trump had previously stated that, at the request of the Iranian government, he would suspend strikes against Iranian energy facilities for 10 days, resuming at 8 p.m. Eastern Time on April 6.

... (Xinhua News Agency) Market Data: As of press time, according to CoinGecko data: BTC price is $66,678.85, a 24-hour change of +1.2%; ETH price is $2,024.14, a 24-hour change of +2.2%; BNB price is $608.29, a 24-hour change of +0.5%. SOL price is $82.45, a 24-hour change of +1.4%; DOGE price is $0.09076, a 24-hour change of +0.6%; XRP price is $1.32, a 24-hour change of -0.1%; TRX price is $0.3193, a 24-hour change of... WLFI price is $0.09867, up +1.1% in 24 hours; HYPE price is $36.78, down -3.2% in 24 hours.

Policy

▌US Department of Labor Proposes Allowing Bitcoin Investment in 401(k) Plans

On March 31, according to a post on social media by Bitcoin Magazine, the US Department of Labor proposed a rule allowing Bitcoin investment in 401(k) plans.

▌Analysis: Senators Expected to Release Final Legislative Text of the Clarity Act This Week

According to Crypto In America, although Congress is in recess for two weeks for Easter, behind-the-scenes work on the Clarity Act is about to accelerate. Senators are expected to release the final legislative text this week, detailing the revised stablecoin yield and incentive compromise, including how cryptocurrency companies can provide incentives without causing deposits to flow out of banks.

The revised draft stems from industry discontent with a previous draft agreed upon by Senators Thom Tillis (Republican, North Carolina) and Angela Althorbush (Democrat, Maryland) and the White House. Several stakeholders, including Coinbase and Stripe, expressed opposition to the draft after reviewing it. Under the previous proposal, companies would be prohibited from offering yields "directly or indirectly" on passive stablecoin balances or anything resembling interest, but activity-based rewards would still be permitted. Given that the Senate Banking Committee is currently scheduled to conduct possible deliberations in the last two weeks of April, this means that industry and lawmakers have approximately three weeks to resolve other remaining issues before pushing the Clarity Act from the committee to the full Senate. Unresolved issues surrounding decentralized finance (DeFi), token classification, and tokenization are expected to influence the final days before Chairman Tim Scott (Republican, South Carolina) schedules a deliberation date.

▌White House: Iran "Duplicates Words," Negotiations Continue

On March 31, White House Press Secretary Levitt stated on Monday that negotiations with Iran are ongoing and progressing well. Levitt also pointed out that there are discrepancies between what Iran publicly states and what it privately tells U.S. officials. Levitt stated, "Although what people hear from the Iranian regime are mostly superficial statements and false reports, negotiations are continuing and progressing well. Of course, there is a significant difference between what is publicly announced and what is conveyed privately."

▌Trump Confirms U.S. is in Dialogue with Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf

On March 31, U.S. President Trump stated in an interview with the New York Post on Monday that Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf is one of the Iranian officials communicating with the U.S. regarding ending the war.

When asked if he could trust Kalibaf in cooperating with Washington, Trump replied, "We'll see." He added that he would inform them of the details in about a week. On Sunday, Trump told the Financial Times that Kalibaf had authorized the passage of oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz. Trump interpreted this move as Kalibaf being willing to make concessions to reach an agreement to end the war. Kalibaf denied having held any negotiations with the United States, but experts said he was a hardliner and had the potential to reach an agreement. According to the Wall Street Journal, Kalibaf was one of two Iranian officials temporarily removed from the U.S. and Israel's "death list."

Blockchain Applications

▌T-RIZE Launches $500 Million Digital Bond Program on Canton Network

On March 30th, T-RIZE Group will build a $500 million private credit digital bond program on Canton Network for Kairos Litigation Limited, a UK SPV under Horizon Group. The initial $50 million share of the program will soon be launched to compliant investors in the US and Europe, with subsequent shares to be increased as needed. The program aims to transform complex private credit assets (underlying assets are UK litigation financing receivables) into digital instruments with fixed income. T-RIZE is responsible for tokenization design, digital issuance architecture, governance control logic, and on-chain tool creation. The architecture employs a bankruptcy remote design, with assets and cash flows surrounded by protection, and capital protection support provided by a Grade A international reinsurance company.

▌Morgan Stanley Launches Low-Cost Bitcoin ETF After NYSE Approval

The New York Stock Exchange has officially approved the listing of Morgan Stanley's Bitcoin spot ETF, which will be traded on the NYSE Arca under the ticker symbol MSBT. The fund will hold actual Bitcoin, with Coinbase handling cold storage custody, and BNY Mellon handling cash management, administration, and transfer functions. MSBT has an annual fee rate of 0.14%, lower than Grayscale's current lowest of 0.15% and BlackRock's IBIT's 0.25%, making it the lowest among all Bitcoin spot ETFs in the US.

▌Upbit's parent company Dunamu's 2025 revenue declined by 10.0% year-on-year

On March 30, according to South Korean media Decenter, Dunamu, the operator of Upbit, South Korea's largest cryptocurrency exchange, released its 2025 annual performance report, showing a decline in both revenue and profit.

... According to its consolidated financial statements, Dunamu's revenue in 2025 was 1.56 trillion won (US$1.028 billion), a 10.0% decrease year-on-year (compared to 1.73 trillion won (US$1.141 billion) in the same period of the previous year); operating profit was 869.3 billion won (US$573 million), a 26.7% decrease year-on-year (compared to 1.18 trillion won (US$778 million) in the same period of the previous year); and net profit was 708.9 billion won (US$467 million), a 27.9% decrease year-on-year (compared to 983.8 billion won (US$653 million) in the same period of the previous year). The report stated that the decline in performance was mainly attributed to the shrinking trading volume in the cryptocurrency market against the backdrop of a global economic downturn. Dunamu's revenue structure is highly concentrated in transaction fees, which accounted for 98.26% of total revenue in 2025.

▌CoinShares: $414 Million Outflow from Digital Asset Investment Products Last Week

According to CoinShares monitoring, digital asset investment products experienced their first outflow of funds in five weeks last week, totaling $414 million. Previously, market expectations for the Federal Reserve's June interest rate meeting had shifted from a rate cut to a rate hike. Global assets under management (AuM) fell to $129 billion, returning to early February levels, roughly equivalent to the level at the initial stage of Trump's tariff policy in April 2025. Ethereum bore the brunt of the negative sentiment, possibly related to news of the Clarity Act, resulting in a $222 million outflow, bringing the year-to-date net outflow to $273 million, the most severe among all digital assets. Bitcoin also experienced a total outflow of $194 million, but still maintains a net inflow of $964 million so far this year, while investment products shorting Bitcoin received $4 million in inflows.

... Solana was also affected, experiencing an outflow of $12.3 million. XRP was one of the few assets that saw an inflow of funds, totaling $15.8 million.

▌Ethereum Foundation Pledges $46.2 Million Worth of ETH

On March 30th, Arkham monitoring showed that the Ethereum Foundation pledged $46.2 million worth of ETH, the largest amount of ETH ever pledged by the Ethereum Foundation.

▌BitMine Increased its Holdings by Over 71,000 ETH Last Week, Bringing its Total Holdings to Over 4.73 Million ETH

As of March 29th, Eastern Time, BitMine's total holdings of cryptocurrency + cash + "Moonshot" totaled $10.7 billion.

BitMine holds 4,732,082 ETH (an increase of 71,179 ETH from last week), representing 3.92% of the total Ethereum supply (120.7 million ETH). It also holds 197 BTC, $200 million worth of shares in Beast Industries, $102 million worth of shares in Eightco Holdings (NASDAQ: ORBS), and $961 million in uncollateralized cash. As of March 29, 2026, BitMine has a total of 3,142,643 ETH staked (worth $6.3 billion at $2,005 per ETH).

▌Coinbase to List EDGEX

On March 31, Coinbase will list EDGEX (EDGEX).

... **Important Economic Developments** **Federal Reserve Governor Milan: The Fed Could Gradually Cut Rates by One Percentage Point Over a Year** On March 30th, Federal Reserve Governor Milan stated that the Fed could gradually cut interest rates by one percentage point over a year. The Fed's balance sheet is too large, and the Fed hopes to shrink it. Rate cuts could offset the balance sheet reduction. **Powell: The Fed's Policy Tools Have No Significant Effect on Supply Shocks** Federal Reserve Chairman Powell stated that the Fed's policy tools have no significant effect on supply shocks. (At the policy level) the Fed tends to ignore supply shocks, but must closely monitor inflation expectations.

▌Powell: Long-term inflation expectations are solid; no rush to deal with energy shock

On March 30, Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell said on Monday that despite the ongoing energy shock, long-term inflation expectations appear to remain stable, and the Fed does not yet need to make a decision on how to deal with the latest situation.

"Inflation expectations outside the short term appear to remain solid," he said at Harvard University. Regarding the energy shock related to the war with Iran, he stated, "We don't yet know its specific impact on the economy, but given that inflation has been consistently above the 2% target level, we will closely monitor its impact on price pressures." On March 30th, according to CCTV, Aladdin Boroujerdi, a member of the National Security and Foreign Policy Committee of the Iranian Islamic Parliament, stated that given the current international security situation and external threats, Iran is seriously considering withdrawing from the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons and plans to implement stricter access and toll systems for ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz. Boroujerdi also stated that Iran currently has complete control over the strategic waterway of the Strait of Hormuz. He revealed that Iran plans to establish a completely new strait management system, modeled after Turkey's management of the Bosphorus Strait and Egypt's management of the Suez Canal, requiring ships passing through the strait to obtain Iranian permission and pay corresponding passage and service fees. On March 31, the Israeli military and other Israeli leaders stated that, one month after the start of the war with Iran, the Israeli military had almost completed bombing all targets it had identified at the beginning of the conflict, and that Israel's military objectives in Iran had entered the "final stage." The Israeli political leadership has ordered the military to shift its focus to targeting the Iranian regime's "economic" objectives. Israeli security sources stated that the end of the war depends on Trump. (CCTV News) On March 31, according to CME's "FedWatch": the probability of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates by 25 basis points in April is 2.6%, and the probability of keeping interest rates unchanged is 97.4%. The probability of the Federal Reserve cumulatively cutting interest rates by 25 basis points by June is 5%, the probability of keeping interest rates unchanged is 92.5%, and the probability of cumulatively raising interest rates by 25 basis points is 2.5%. Golden Encyclopedia Can Bitcoin still be called "digital gold" under geopolitical crises? Every time there is a geopolitical crisis, gold prices rise and Bitcoin prices plummet. After six tests, the claim of "digital gold" has never been confirmed by data. Countries hoard gold but exclude Bitcoin from their reserves. For investors, Bitcoin exhibits an asymmetry: it falls with stocks but doesn't rise with them. Three structural asymmetries prevent Bitcoin from achieving safe-haven status: an overabundance of derivatives (market structure), the dominance of leveraged traders (participant composition), and a lack of repeatable behavioral records (behavioral accumulation). Bitcoin is not a safe-haven asset, but it is a "crisis-useful asset," capable of functioning when borders are closed and banks fail. If these three asymmetries narrow, Bitcoin may no longer be a replica of gold but a completely new "next-generation gold." Generational shifts and the widespread adoption of algorithms are key factors that could accelerate this process.

data.title: $PUMP Valuation Breakdown: Where Does the Real Discount Come From?
data.description:
Author: Max Wong @IOSG

Introduction

Pump.fun launched as a permissionless meme launchpad on Solana in early 2024, allowing anyone to create and trade tokens in seconds via the Bonding Curve mechanism. The project started as a niche experiment but quickly became one of the highest-grossing applications on public blockchains.

Between 2024 and 2025, Pump.fun's average daily protocol revenue consistently matched or even surpassed Hyperliquid's, and the inherently cyclical nature of the meme market makes this figure even more noteworthy.

The native token $PUMP was issued at $0.004 through a $600 million ICO, with a FDV of $4 billion. Over the past few months, revenue has hit record highs and the token value has doubled, but $PUMP is currently priced at approximately $0.0019, down about 80% from its all-time high of $0.086 (corresponding to an FDV of $8.6 billion). Its current market capitalization is approximately $679 million, with an FDV of $1.9 billion. The gap between revenue trends and valuation is significant. This report reviews Pump.fun's product evolution and ecosystem strategy, conducts stress tests on its revenue to determine whether it is inflated, and judges whether the current valuation is a pricing deviation or a reasonable discount to the real risks. Pump.fun is no longer just a launchpad. Starting in late 2024, it began expanding into peripheral businesses, broadening its revenue streams, and deepening its control over on-chain speculative traffic. Launchpad (core product) was the earliest product and the starting point for brand recognition. Anyone can deploy tokens by paying a small fee.

PumpSwap

PumpSwap is an AMM DEX built by Pump.fun, launched in March 2025, with a very direct purpose: to reclaim the graduation fees that previously flowed to Raydium (Raydium charges 6 SOL for each graduation token). Following the fee update in May 2025, the protocol will take 0.05% of each transaction, LPs will receive 0.20%, and token issuers will receive 0.05%. Features include: creating liquidity pools for any token for free, injecting liquidity into existing pools, and trading all PumpSwap listed tokens. Padre / Pump Terminal was acquired by Pump.fun and renamed Terminal. It is positioned as a professional trading terminal and currently supports Solana, BNB, Base, and ETH. Similar to other terminals, it features: Trenches (view newly migrated/soon-to-be-migrated tokens), a customizable interface, flash sales and instant buy-ins, multi-wallet strategies, and a binding detector. Pumplive is a live streaming feature within the platform, allowing streamers to associate a token when creating a live stream. The logic is "the publisher is the exchange," similar to the models of Parti and Kick/stake.com: broadcasters want to drive trading volume because they take a cut of the total fees; token holders want more trading volume and buying pressure. The more broadcasters broadcast, the more active the token becomes, and the greater the trading volume.

Ecosystem Initiatives

Since TGE, Pump.fun has approximately $1 billion in cash reserves, continuously launching new product lines (the acquisition of Padre is one example), and is also doing several things:

Pumpfund

2026 The $3 million BiP (Build in Public) hackathon launched on January 19th. Based on a $10 million valuation, it provided $250,000 in funding to each of 12 projects. The selection criteria leaned towards market-driven selection based on public attention, rather than the traditional VC review process. Glass Full Foundation (GFF) is a liquidity injection program launched in August 2025. Through 5 transparent wallets, it deployed approximately $1.7 million (2,022 SOL) to 10 tokens (including Tokabu 21.3%, House 20.6%, USDUC, NEET, MASK, FART, etc.), selecting projects with high community engagement. Project Ascend, launched in 2025, is a creator incentive program with a core feature of dynamically tiered creator fees (0.95% to 0.05%). Its goal is to increase creator earnings tenfold while accelerating the CTO (Community Takeover) application process. The table below summarizes the three product lines. 2025 data represents actual performance, while 2026 data represents projected performance rates. Currently, approximately 32.7% of the platform's total revenue comes from non-Launchpad products, demonstrating that revenue diversification is beginning to yield results.

▲ Pumpfun trading volume chart

▲ Pumpswap Trading Volume Chart

▲ Padre/Pump Terminal Trading Volume Chart

Does Pump.fun have inflated trading volumes?

$PUMP's fundamentals appear strong on the surface, but the core question is: does the trading volume truly reflect real economic activity, or is it being inflated by users and bots?

$PUMP's fundamentals seem strong on the surface, but the core question is: does the trading volume reflect real economic activity, or is it being inflated by users and bots? **Trading Volume Correlation Analysis** The logic is simple: In a natural market, Launchpad and PumpSwap trading volumes should be positively correlated with a time lag. Launchpad activity indicates high genuine speculative interest, with some funds flowing into PumpSwap through the graduation mechanism to support post-listing trading. However, if there is severe market manipulation, this relationship will break. Launchpad trading volume is artificially inflated, with tokens graduating based on fabricated curve activity, resulting in no real buyers entering PumpSwap. The result is a surge in Launchpad volume, while PumpSwap volume remains flat or even declines, causing the correlation to approach zero or turn negative. The most telling combination of signals is: a surge in the graduation rate (more tokens artificially reaching the curve threshold), while the trading volume of a single token on PumpSwap is low and rapidly declining, and PumpSwap liquidity depth does not increase in tandem with the number of graduated tokens. Data from January 2026 to present: (The first two data points are anomalies due to PumpSwap fee and market maker policy adjustments and are not included in the correlation analysis.)

Discovery:

Launchpad trading volume is stable, fluctuating between $400 million and $570 million over 8 weeks (approximately 40% range). This is not surprising considering that many bundlers and users engaging in wash trading are maintaining a minimum trading volume.

PumpSwap is more volatile, ranging between $3.5 billion and $5.8 billion over the same period (approximately 60% range), primarily driven by a surge in meme trading demand in mid-January and additional incentives for the team, but Launchpad did not see a corresponding increase in volume.

PumpSwap is more volatile, ranging between $3.5 billion and $5.8 billion over the same period (approximately 60% range), mainly driven by a surge in meme trading demand in mid-January and additional incentives for the team, but Launchpad did not see a corresponding increase in volume.

r = 0.579, moderately positive correlation. With a sample size n=8, p<0.05 requires r>0.63, which does not reach the significance threshold, but the direction and strength are consistent with the organic growth hypothesis. University of Pisa paper: Researchers at the University of Pisa conducted a comprehensive on-chain analysis of Pump.fun Launchpad, covering all transactions of 655,770 tokens issued between September and October 2025, distinguishing between bot and human transactions using Solana transaction log metadata. Four of the findings directly relate to the issue of fraudulent transactions. Large-scale manual buy-ins are the strongest predictor of graduation. The strongest predictive signal for graduation is the rapid accumulation of SOL through a small number of large-scale transactions. The median number of successful graduations is only about 457, taking approximately 4.4 minutes from token creation to graduation. This pattern (large, low-frequency capital injections from different wallets) is consistent with coordinated artificial speculation (Telegram group calls for orders, KOL hype) or continuous pump-and-dump schemes, not high-frequency trading bots artificially inflating volume. Instead, bot-dominated tokens accumulate a large number of small transactions and then stagnate before graduation. Bot activity actually inhibits graduation. After the early curve phase, the probability of bot-active tokens graduating is systematically lower. At that time, graduation required accumulating approximately 85 SOLs on the curve. If bots were artificially inflating volume to rush graduation, the graduation rate of bot-active tokens should be higher, but the data shows the opposite. The reason is structural: upon graduation, the Bonding Curve transitions from virtual reserves to real AMM reserves, and the effective liquidity depth decreases discretely. Selling before graduation (at the depth supported by virtual reserves) is more profitable than selling after graduation. The research also found that the top ten token issuers in September 2025, each issuing over 2,000 tokens in a single month, exhibited statistically anomalous sell sequences initiated by wallet clusters before each token reached the graduation threshold. Bundled traders and snipers built positions early, then sold off during the upward curve attracting retail demand. The paper concludes that most bots on the platform are frontrunners, extracting value from human trading counterparts at entry and exit points, not wash traders trying to reach the graduation threshold. Bots buy/hoard large supplies and then dump them on retail investors near graduation. This is different from wash trading. The paper calculates the net SOL flow for the complete dataset (total SOL used for the curve minus the total SOL withdrawn from sales). During the single-month observation period, the ecosystem accumulated a net retention of approximately 160,000 SOL (equivalent to approximately $32 million at September 2025 prices). This is a hard test of wash trading: the circulating transaction volume between related wallets leads to a near-zero net capital flow because buying and selling offset each other. The $32 million net retention is structurally incompatible with the large-scale circulating trading volume, indicating a continuous inflow of genuine external retail capital into Launchpad. The 1.25% transaction fee per trade generates losses, funding protocol revenue. The paper's findings are consistent with our volume correlation analysis: the large volume of trading on Launchpad is generated by tethered traders and snipers pumping and dumping, forming a volume floor, but it is not wash trading. The key difference is that wash trading generates zero net protocol revenue (fees between linked wallets offset each other), while pump-and-dump generates real fees on each trade (from real retail counterparties who pay the platform). The approximately $390 million in ARR confirms that the platform monetizes real retail trading volume through its pump-and-dump ecosystem, rather than creating fake metrics. Currently, the Pump Foundation uses 100% of all revenue from all product lines to buy back $PUMP on the open market. Since announcing its 100% revenue buyback on July 15, 2025, in 8 months:

It has bought back 27% of the circulating supply, clearing 9.6% of the total supply.

In comparison: Hyperliquid, since initiating its buyback program in November 2024, has only burned 4.1% of the total supply (approximately 12.3% of the circulating supply). Based on current prices and revenue, the annualized liquidation ratio is close to 45%.

Supply Structure and Unlocking

Total Supply: 1,000,000,000,000 PUMP

Circulating Supply: 430,000,000,000 (43%)

Remaining Locked: Approximately 58% of Total Supply

Major Unlocking Nodes: In Progress: 12% (as of July, monthly 2% allocated to community and incentives) July 2026: 8.25% unlocked, followed by 0.68% per month for the next 36 months. Valuation Analysis: If the wash trading analysis holds true, $PUMP is undervalued and has asymmetric upside potential. The discount stems from three aspects: The market doubts the sustainability of revenue. The market believes that Pump.fun's overall trading volume is speculative, cyclical, and tied to short-term meme activities. Investors treat current profitability as temporary. At the current P/E ratio, buybacks have a financial synergistic effect, but this is not included in the valuation model because the underlying assumption is that revenue will be significantly compressed. The crux of the debate isn't whether Pump.fun is profitable now, but whether it will be profitable in 24 months. We interviewed 15 Tier 1 funds and VCs to understand their views on $PUMP. Only one of the 15 actively tracks $PUMP using bottom-up analysis. Most institutions haven't modeled the new product suite, haven't broken down revenue by product line, and haven't stress-tested the sustainability of trading volume. This lack of coverage creates a narrative vacuum, with pricing driven more by market perception than financial analysis. In contrast, $HYPE has deeper institutional backing, more research coverage, and a clearer product positioning, supporting a higher and more stable valuation multiple. There's also a self-reinforcing effect: assets associated with meme infrastructure are implicitly categorized as speculative and ephemeral, and trading activity follows suit. The market needs time and data across multiple cycles to update this cognitive framework. Valuation compression may persist, regardless of current cash flow, until Pump's revenue withstands a broader crypto market correction and institutional coverage expands. Management trust has not yet been established. Investor concerns focus on: a long-term vision beyond the meme, capital allocation discipline, product roadmap execution, and the transition from viral growth to a sustainable platform economy. The market typically assigns lower valuation multiples to founder-led high-growth platforms until they demonstrate resilience during market volatility, proving that growth can translate into a sustainable platform economy. This discount is likely to persist until Pump demonstrates continued revenue diversification and robust execution through products like PumpSwap and Pump Terminal.

OpenAI's spending cuts trigger a chain reaction: Global memory chip prices begin to fall. On March 30th, according to Bianews, as concerns about OpenAI's cash flow and expansion capabilities intensified, the tech giant's recent spending cuts directly dealt a heavy blow to the previously booming memory chip market. It was OpenAI's explosive growth that had previously triggered the surge in memory prices. As an indispensable core component in consumer electronics and data centers, suppliers' production capacity simply could not meet the massive demand from the AI ​​industry. Data from market research firm TrendForce shows that memory prices have skyrocketed by about seven times in the past year. However, as OpenAI begins to put the brakes on its massive data center investments, this price tsunami is finally showing signs of abating. For example, some DDR5 memory kits have seen price reductions of up to $100. According to follow-up reports from industry media, a 32GB memory module on Amazon... The price of the capacity kit has fallen sharply from its recent high of $490 to $370. However, as the creator behind ChatGPT, OpenAI has recently halted several extremely expensive star projects, leading the market to question whether they can still absorb so many chips. Just last week, in order to control ever-increasing operating costs, they shut down the once-promising AI video generation application Sora. Earlier this month, they also tore up a multi-billion dollar partnership with Oracle and abandoned plans to expand their Texas super data center.

Lista DAO Launches New Proposal: Introducing LISTA Token Economic Model 2.0, Gradually Phased Out veLISTA Mechanism On March 30th, Lista DAO released Proposal LIP024, proposing to introduce LISTA token economic model 2.0, gradually phase out the veLISTA mechanism, and expand the application of LISTA throughout the protocol. The voting period for this proposal is from March 30th to April 2nd, 2026. Key changes include: 1. Simplifying governance; holding LISTA grants voting eligibility for proposals. The liquidity provider (LP) pool voting mechanism will be gradually phased out. 3. Replacing revenue sharing with LISTA buybacks. Fees previously allocated to veLISTA stakers will be reinvested in protocol development, user benefits, and LISTA buybacks. 4.
